edges.description: Constitutively active RAS and the PAX8-PPARG fusion drive sustained downstream proliferative signaling.
Sustained mitogenic signaling drives clonal expansion of transformed follicular cells.
The proliferating follicular neoplasm breaches its capsule and invades vessels, the defining malignant feature that separates carcinoma from adenoma.
Vascular invasion provides the route for hematogenous dissemination to distant organs, particularly bone and lung.

nodes.description: Capsular and/or vascular invasion is the histopathologic hallmark that defines follicular thyroid carcinoma and distinguishes it from the benign follicular adenoma. The degree of vascular invasion (minimally invasive, encapsulated angioinvasive, widely invasive) is a key prognostic determinant.

evidence.reference_title: Preliminary machine-learning model with clinical, US, and CEUS features for the diagnosis of thyroid follicular-patterned lesions.
evidence.snippet: Though follicular thyroid carcinoma can be confirmed postoperatively by the histological findings of capsular or vascular invasion

evidence.reference_title: Prognostic factors of papillary and follicular carcinomas based on pre-, intra-, and post-operative findings.
evidence.snippet: FTCs are classified as minimally invasive, encapsulated angioinvasive, and widely invasive FTCs.

nodes.description: Persistent MAPK and PI3K/AKT signaling promotes uncontrolled proliferation of transformed thyroid follicular cells, producing an encapsulated follicular-patterned neoplasm.

evidence.snippet: The cytoarchitectural features of FTC are similar to those of follicular adenoma (FA), and it is difficult to preoperatively distinguish between FA and FTC.

nodes.description: Following vascular invasion, follicular thyroid carcinoma disseminates predominantly through the bloodstream rather than via lymphatics, with the most frequent distant metastatic sites being bone and lung. This hematogenous pattern distinguishes follicular carcinoma from the lymphatically spreading papillary carcinoma.

evidence.reference_title: [Follicular thyroid carcinoma metastatic to the lung and bone and cutaneous: one case report].
evidence.snippet: Follicular thyroid carcinoma usually metastasizes through a hematogenous route, the most frequent sites of distant metastases are bone and lung

evidence.reference_title: Chest wall metastasis in postoperative thyroid cancer: a case report.
evidence.snippet: thyroid follicular carcinomas are mostly observed in hematogenous metastases

nodes.description: RAS-family mutations and PI3K-pathway lesions co-activate the MAPK (RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK) and PI3K/AKT cascades. In follicular thyroid carcinoma the PI3K/AKT arm is especially prominent, promoting cell growth, survival, metabolism, and invasive behavior, while moderate "RAS-like" MAPK output is associated with the follicular architecture.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular Markers Involved in Tumorigenesis of Thyroid Carcinoma: Focus on Aggressive Histotypes.
evidence.snippet: Genetic rearrangements in TCDFC (RET/PTC, PAX8/PPARG) and mutations in RAS, BRAF, TERT promoter (TERTp), TP53, PIK3CA, and AKT1 are discussed.

nodes.description: Early oncogenic events in follicular thyroid carcinoma are dominated by activating point mutations in the RAS family (NRAS, HRAS, KRAS) and by the PAX8-PPARG gene fusion arising from a t(2;3)(q13;p25) translocation. These truncal alterations occur in thyroid follicular cells and define the "RAS-like" molecular class of follicular-patterned thyroid tumors. Mutant RAS is locked in the active GTP-bound state, and the chimeric PAX8-PPARG protein exhibits neomorphic transcriptional activity dysregulating differentiation and metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: Changes in the clinicopathological characteristics and genetic alterations of follicular thyroid cancer.
evidence.snippet: In 134 samples, genetic tests for N/H/KRAS and TERT promoter mutations and PAX8/PPARγ rearrangement were performed.
